title: Dadasaheb Phalke Award and 71st Winners
focus: Details the history of India's highest film honour and lists the major winners of the 71st National Film Awards.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 112]
What is Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
* It is part of the National Film Awards, a highly coveted collection of honours in the film industry.
* The Award is named after Dhundiraj Govind Phalke, the pioneering filmmaker who gave India its first film– ‘Raja Harishchandra’, in 1913.
* It is awarded for “its outstanding contribution to the growth and development of Indian cinema.
* The award was instituted by the government in 1969 and consists of a ‘Swarna Kamal’, a cash prize of Rs 10 lakh, a certificate, a silk roll, and a shawl.
* The President of India presents the award.
* The first recipient of the award was Devika Rani Roerich in 1969.
* Recently it has been awarded to Mohanlal

[Page 113]
Best Feature Film
Movie: 12th Fail
Director: Vidhu Vinod Chopra
Tagline: Haar Nahi Maanunga (I will not give up)
Award Winners:
- Shah Rukh Khan: Best Actor
- Vikrant Massey: Best Actor
- 12th Fail: Feature Film
- Rani Mukerji: Best Actress
